parts.contentJson:
{"text":"Design a Google Chrome browser extension sidebar panel for a women's health personalization tool called "Luma." The sidebar is injected into the right side of the browser window, approximately 320px wide and full browser height. It sits on top of page content with a clean white card surface and a subtle left border.
The sidebar has three sections stacked vertically:
1. Header (top)
A slim top bar with the "Luma" wordmark in soft purple on the left, a toggle switch on the right to turn the extension on/off, and a small minimize chevron icon. Underneath, a muted one-line status label that reads "Analyzing page for you…" with a subtle animated loading dot.
2. Relevance card (middle)
A rounded card with a colored relevance pill at the top — label reads "High relevance · PCOS" in purple. Below that, 2–3 lines of personalized insight text in dark gray, e.g. "This study focuses on insulin resistance in women with PCOS ages 18–35 — directly relevant to your profile." Below the text, a small row of demographic caveat tags in light gray pill style: "Study: women only", "Ages 18–40", "N=200". At the bottom of the card, a small "Ask a follow-up →" text link in purple.
3. Recent activity strip (bottom)
A compact section titled "Your recent searches" in small all-caps muted label style. Below it, 3 rows of recent search chips with a small search icon, e.g. "PCOS and metformin", "endometriosis pain management", "best diet for hormonal balance." Each row has a faint right-side relevance dot (green, yellow, or gray) indicating how relevant that search was.
Style: Clean, minimal, medical-adjacent but warm. White background, soft purple (#7F77DD) as primary accent, light gray borders, Inter or DM Sans font. Rounded corners throughout (12px). No harsh shadows — use very light elevation (2px blur, 4% opacity). Feels like a trusted health companion, not a clinical tool.","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"I'll create a Chrome extension sidebar panel for Luma with the three sections you described.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"text":"The user wants me to design a Chrome extension sidebar panel for a women's health personalization tool called "Luma". Let me break down the requirements:

1. **Dimensions**: 320px wide, full browser height
2. **Three sections**:
   - Header with logo, toggle, minimize button, and status
   - Relevance card with colored pill, insight text, demographic tags, and a link
   - Recent activity strip with search chips

3. **Style**: Clean, minimal, medical-adjacent but warm. White background, soft purple (#7F77DD), light gray borders, Inter or DM Sans font, 12px rounded corners, very light shadows.

This is a React + Tailwind application. I need to:
1. Read the existing App.tsx file
2. Create the sidebar component with all three sections
3. Use Tailwind classes for styling
4. Add some animation for the loading dot
5. Make sure it's responsive and matches the design requirements

Let me start by reading the current App.tsx file and then creating the component.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Good, now I have the context. Let me create the Luma sidebar extension panel. I'll need to:

1. Create the main component in App.tsx with all three sections
2. Add the soft purple color (#7F77DD) as specified
3. Use Inter or DM Sans font (I'll check if these are available or add them)
4. Implement the loading animation for the status
5. Create the three sections: Header, Relevance Card, and Recent Activity Strip

Let me write the complete implementation.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"I've created a Chrome extension sidebar panel for Luma with a clean, medical-adjacent design. The panel features a working toggle switch, animated loading dots in the status bar, a relevance card with demographic tags, and a recent searches section with color-coded relevance indicators. The design uses the soft purple (#7F77DD) accent color, Inter font, rounded corners, and very subtle shadows as specified.","textMode":"visible_message"}
{"text":"Perfect! Now let me verify that the dev server is running and the application looks correct. Let me check if there's a running dev server.","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"include a chatbot section at the bottom where people can ask follow up questions, be directed to other similar sources, etc.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"The user wants me to add a chatbot section at the bottom of the sidebar for follow-up questions and getting directed to similar sources. I should update the App.tsx file to include this chatbot interface at the bottom.

I'll need to:

2. Make it look clean and consistent with the existing design
3. Keep the Luma style - soft purple accents, rounded corners, etc.

Let me read the current App.tsx to see what I need to modify.","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"Paste this at the top of any Figma prompt:

Visual style: Clean, modern, and quietly feminine. The design should feel like a premium wellness brand — think Glossier meets Linear. Polished and professional, never cutesy or overly soft.
Color palette: Primary background is pure white (#FFFFFF). Primary accent is a muted mauve-purple (#9B8EC4) — used for active states, pills, and key labels. Secondary accent is a dusty rose (#E8A5A5) — used sparingly for highlights, hover states, or warm tags only. All other UI elements use a cool neutral gray scale: #F5F4F2 for surface backgrounds, #E2E0DC for borders, #9E9B96 for secondary text, #2C2B28 for primary text. No pure black anywhere.
Typography: DM Sans for all UI text. Headings at 13–14px medium (500 weight). Body and labels at 12px regular. All caps + wide letter-spacing (0.08em) only for section labels and microlabels. Never bold-heavy — the hierarchy comes from size and color contrast, not weight.
Shape language: 12px border radius on cards and inputs. 999px (fully round) for pills and tags. No hard corners anywhere. Thin 1px borders in #E2E0DC — no drop shadows except a single ultra-subtle one: 0px 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) on floating panels only.
Femininity through restraint: The femininity should come from the palette (mauve, dusty rose, warm neutrals) and generous whitespace — not from decorative elements, florals, or rounded bubbly type. Think editorial, not illustrative. The result should look equally at home in a medical context as a beauty app.
Spacing: 24px outer padding on all panels. 16px between sections. 8px between related elements. Breathe — never crowd.
Icons: 16px stroke-style icons, 1.5px stroke weight, rounded caps. Phosphor Icons or Lucide style.


Three color combos to tell Figma explicitly if it needs hex values:
ElementColorBackground#FFFFFFSurface / card fill#F5F4F2Border#E2E0DCPrimary accent (purple)#9B8EC4Warm accent (rose)#E8A5A5Primary text#2C2B28Secondary text#9E9B96Success dot#7FC69B

The key phrase that gets Figma AI to nail the vibe is "Glossier meets Linear" — it immediately pulls it toward that warm-but-minimal, feminine-but-technical sweet spot. You can also try "Notion meets Flo Health" for something slightly more utilitarian.","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"I'll save this design system to memory so it's applied to all future Figma-related work.","textMode":"visible_message"}

{"toolCallId":"toolu_vrtx_01BcmjtT3T9izBB69LmiFAhE","toolName":"write_tool","argsJson":"{\"path\":\"/workspaces/default/sessions/21d31702-c95b-4f4a-a3e4-8e982377f496/claude/projects/-workspaces-default-code/memory/feedback_figma_design_system.md\",\"file_text\":\"---\\nname: Figma Design System - Luma Visual Style\\ndescription: Complete design system for Luma and similar Figma projects - \\\"Glossier meets Linear\\\" aesthetic\\ntype: feedback\\n---\\n\\nUse this design system for all Figma-related design work, especially for Luma or similar women's health/wellness applications.\\n\\n**Visual style:** Clean, modern, and quietly feminine. Premium wellness brand aesthetic — think Glossier meets Linear (or \\\"Notion meets Flo Health\\\" for more utilitarian). Polished and professional, never cutesy or overly soft.\\n\\n**Color palette:**\\n- Primary background: pure white (#FFFFFF)\\n- Primary accent: muted mauve-purple (#9B8EC4) — active states, pills, key labels\\n- Secondary accent: dusty rose (#E8A5A5) — highlights, hover states, warm tags only (use sparingly)\\n- Cool neutral gray scale:\\n  - #F5F4F2 for surface backgrounds\\n  - #E2E0DC for borders\\n  - #9E9B96 for secondary text\\n  - #2C2B28 for primary text\\n- Success/status dots: #7FC69B\\n- No pure black anywhere\\n\\n**Typography:** \\n- DM Sans for all UI text\\n- Headings: 13–14px medium (500 weight)\\n- Body and labels: 12px regular\\n- All caps + wide letter-spacing (0.08em) only for section labels and microlabels\\n- Never bold-heavy — hierarchy comes from size and color contrast, not weight\\n\\n**Shape language:**\\n- 12px border radius on cards and inputs\\n- 999px (fully round) for pills and tags\\n- No hard corners anywhere\\n- Thin 1px borders in #E2E0DC\\n- No drop shadows except: 0px 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) on floating panels only\\n\\n**Femininity through restraint:** The femininity comes from palette (mauve, dusty rose, warm neutrals) and generous whitespace — not from decorative elements, florals, or rounded bubbly type. Think editorial, not illustrative. Should look equally at home in medical context as beauty app.\\n\\n**Spacing:**\\n- 24px outer padding on all panels\\n- 16px between sections\\n- 8px between related elements\\n- Breathe — never crowd\\n\\n**Icons:** 16px stroke-style icons, 1.5px stroke weight, rounded caps. Phosphor Icons or Lucide style.\\n\\n**Why:** User specified this exact design language to create a warm-but-minimal, feminine-but-technical aesthetic for health/wellness applications.\\n\\n**How to apply:** Use these exact values when building UI. The key phrase \\\"Glossier meets Linear\\\" captures the vibe. Apply restraint — femininity through palette and space, not decoration.\\n\"}","providerOptions":{}}
